rulings.Case Title: LORAN SIMON VS. TODD BRABEC ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice And Motion To Compel Defendant Deborah Rosenthals Response To Demand For Inspection And Copying Of Documents And Things, Set No. Three, And Request For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Set for Law and Motion/Discovery Calendar on Tuesday, November 25, 2025, Line 1. PLAINTIFF LORAN SIMON's Motion To Compel Defendant Deborah Rosenthals Response To Demand For Inspection And Copying Of Documents And Things, Set No. Three, And Request For Sanctions. Plaintiff Loran Simon's Motion To Compel Defendant Deborah Rosenthal's Response To Demand For Inspection And Copying Of Documents And Things, Set No. Three, And Request For Sanctions is GRANTED IN PART AND DENIED IN PART. (The Court's complete tentative ruling has been emailed to the parties). For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 302 Zoom ID 160 409 7690; Passcode 516287.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/JMQ)

rulings.Case Title: JAMES HINE VS. UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA SAN FRANCISCO ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or, In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication
rulings.Rulings: Set for Law and Motion/Discovery Calendar on Tuesday, November 25, 2025, Line 6. DEFENDANTs MICHAEL CLUNE, SAM HAWGOOD, and THE REGENTS OF THE UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or, In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication. Defendants University of California San Francisco, University of California - SF, Regents of the University of California, Michael Clune, and Sam Hawgood's motion for summary judgment, or in the alternative summary adjudication, is GRANTED IN PART AND DENIED IN PART. (The Court's complete tentative ruling has been emailed to the parties). rulings.Case Title: EMA BELL VS. MAGENTA, INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO CONSOLIDATE ACTIONS OF CASE(S) CGC-24-619680, CGC-25-621457 WITH CASE CGC-24-614344
rulings.Rulings: Set for Law and Motion/Discovery Calendar on Tuesday, November 25, 2025, Line 2. PLAINTIFF EMA BELL's MOTION TO CONSOLIDATE ACTIONS OF CASE(S) CGC-24-619680, CGC-25-621457 WITH CASE CGC-24-614344. Plaintiff Ema Bell's MOTION TO CONSOLIDATE ACTIONS OF CASE(S) CGC-24-619680, CGC-25-621457 WITH CASE CGC-24-614344 is GRANTED. Good cause appearing, the court orders the three captioned cases consolidated and grants Moving Plaintiff's request for leave to file a consolidated complaint. CGC-24-614344 shall be the lead case. The cases are consolidated for all purposes. Moving Plaintiff must file his consolidated complaint, substantially identical to the attachment to the stipulation forthwith but no later than December 9, 2025. Prior to the hearing time, Moving Party shall lodge by email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org a proposed order quoting the text above verbatim. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 302 Zoom ID 160 409 7690; Passcode 516287.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts.
